SEARCH
Package org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node

Interface MapValueNode<K,V>

Type Parameters:
K - The key type.
V - The value type.
All Superinterfaces:
Node, ValueNode<Map<K,V>>
All Known Implementing Classes:
MapConfigNode
@AvailableSince("5.7") public interface MapValueNode<K,V> extends ValueNode<Map<K,V>>
A ValueNode specialization that stores a Map of key-value pairs.
Since:
5.7

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Map.Entry<K,V>
    deserializeEntry(@Nullable Object key, @Nullable Object value)
    Deserializes a raw key-value pair into a map entry.

    serializeEntry(K key, V value)
    Serializes a map entry into raw configuration objects.

    validateEntry(K key, V value)
    Validates a key-value entry.

    Validates a single map value.

    Methods inherited from interface org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.Node

    getPath

    Methods inherited from interface org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ValueNode

    deserialize, getAliases, getDefaultValue, getName, getSerializer, getType, onChange, onLoad, onLoadAndChange, onSetValue, parseFromString, parseFromString, serialize, suggest, suggest, validate

  • Method Details

    • validateKey

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull @NotNull io.vavr.control.Try<Void> validateKey(@Nullable K key)
      Validates a single map key.
      Implementations should return a successful Try when the key is acceptable, or a failed Try when the key is invalid.
      Parameters:
      key - The key to validate, or null if the key is absent.
      Returns:
      A successful Try if the key is valid, or a failed Try if it is not.
      Since:
      5.7

    • validateValue

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull @NotNull io.vavr.control.Try<Void> validateValue(@Nullable V value)
      Validates a single map value.
      Implementations should return a successful Try when the value is acceptable, or a failed Try when the value is invalid.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to validate, or null if the value is absent.
      Returns:
      A successful Try if the value is valid, or a failed Try if it is not.
      Since:
      5.7

    • validateEntry

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull @NotNull io.vavr.control.Try<Void> validateEntry(@Nullable K key, @Nullable V value)
      Validates a key-value entry.
      This is typically implemented by composing validateKey(Object) and validateValue(Object). If either part fails, the entry is considered invalid.
      Parameters:
      key - The entry key, or null if the key is absent.
      value - The entry value, or null if the value is absent.
      Returns:
      A successful Try if both parts are valid, or a failed Try if either part is invalid.
      Since:
      5.7

    • deserializeEntry

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull Map.Entry<K,V> deserializeEntry(@Nullable @Nullable Object key, @Nullable @Nullable Object value)
      Deserializes a raw key-value pair into a map entry.
      This method converts a single persisted entry, not the full map. If either component cannot be converted, implementations may return null for that side of the entry.
      Parameters:
      key - The raw key object, or null if no key is present.
      value - The raw value object, or null if no value is present.
      Returns:
      A map entry containing the deserialized key and value.
      Since:
      5.7

    • serializeEntry

      @AvailableSince("5.7") @NotNull Map.Entry<Object,Object> serializeEntry(@Nullable K key, @Nullable V value)
      Serializes a map entry into raw configuration objects.
      This method converts a single entry to a persisted representation, not the full map. The returned entry contains the serialized key and serialized value.
      Parameters:
      key - The entry key, or null if the key is absent.
      value - The entry value, or null if the value is absent.
      Returns:
      A map entry containing the serialized key and value.
      Since:
      5.7
